prompt:
A highly realistic, cinematic photograph of a small tailor workshop.

At the center, a mannequin is wrapped in an extremely wrinkled, irregular, crumpled fabric form — unnatural folds and bumps everywhere.

A tailor is intensely stitching a suit directly onto this mannequin, carefully tracing every tiny wrinkle and imperfection with obsessive precision.

The suit itself is grotesquely over-detailed: excessive seams, unnecessary stitching lines, exaggerated contours that follow every tiny bump in the mannequin.

Nearby, on a hanger, a simple, clean, well-shaped suit contrasts sharply with the distorted one being made.

Lighting: warm, focused workshop lighting with deep shadows, highlighting the texture of the fabric and stitches.

Mood: obsessive, meticulous, slightly unsettling.

Composition: single frame, no text, no labels, no symbols, no diagrams.

Style: ultra-realistic, high detail, shallow depth of field, 50mm lens look, magazine photography.

Negative prompt: no charts, no graphs, no equations, no text, no glowing AI elements, no futuristic style, no abstract shapes
metaphor: A tailor obsessively fits a suit to every wrinkle of a distorted mannequin, making it unusable for normal shapes—mirroring memorization without generalization.
